nodes.name: skeletonization
nodes.description: Thin binary road masks to centerlines and convert centerline pixels into a traversable graph.
nodes.conceptual_summary: Spatial mask thinning followed by optional graph extraction for road-network pipelines.
nodes.decomposition_rationale: The research separates raster thinning from graph extraction because they have different input and output types.

nodes.name: skeletonize_2d
nodes.description: Reduce a binary 2D mask to a one-pixel-wide skeleton using the Zhang thinning method.

outputs.name: result
outputs.constraints: Binary skeleton with the same shape as mask and no foreground outside mask.

nodes.conceptual_summary: Deterministic 2D morphological thinning for road-mask centerline extraction.
nodes.decomposition_rationale: This is the primary skeletonization atom recommended by the research packet.

nodes.name: medial_axis_2d
nodes.description: Compute a deterministic 2D medial axis skeleton and optionally return its distance map.

inputs.name: return_distance
inputs.constraints: Whether to return the distance transform alongside the skeleton.

inputs.name: rng_seed
inputs.constraints: Nonnegative seed for deterministic medial-axis tie-breaking.

outputs.name: result
outputs.constraints: Skeleton, or skeleton plus nonnegative distance map, preserving mask shape.

nodes.type_signature: (mask: NDArray[np.bool_] | NDArray[np.integer], return_distance: bool = False, rng_seed: int = 42) -> NDArray[np.bool_] | tuple[NDArray[np.bool_], NDArray[np.float64]]
nodes.conceptual_summary: Distance-ridge skeleton extraction with explicit deterministic seed control.
nodes.critic_notes: The research notes medial-axis sensitivity to boundary noise; this atom is secondary to skeletonize_2d.
nodes.decomposition_rationale: Medial axis returns useful width information and has different determinism controls from Zhang thinning.

nodes.name: skeleton_to_graph
nodes.description: Convert a binary centerline skeleton into a NetworkX MultiGraph with node centroids and weighted pixel paths.

inputs.name: skeleton
inputs.constraints: Binary 2D skeleton with at least one foreground pixel.

outputs.name: result
outputs.constraints: Graph nodes have pts and o attributes; graph edges have pts and weight attributes.

nodes.conceptual_summary: Endpoint and junction clustering followed by degree-two chain tracing.
nodes.critic_notes: The local implementation avoids the optional sknw dependency while preserving the graph contract needed by the research stage.
nodes.decomposition_rationale: Graph extraction has a graph output and is separated from raster skeletonization.
